review_comment:
I wasn't aware of `HW_ADDR_TO_STR()`. The trick with a temporary buffer is neat. I wasn't sure if it is valid, but it seems so. At least for C++ it seems that this is well defined: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/37946437/when-exactly-does-the-destruction-of-a-temporary-object-happen-in-a-function-cal says
>  the temporary is destroyed after the end of the full-expression containing it

And the "full-expression" is the function call (http://eel.is/c++draft/intro.execution#5, the outermost function call in case of nested calls due to 5.6).

I hope C follows the same rules.

Anyway, it's not directly related to this PR.
